sanskrit: अमी च त्वां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राः सर्वे सहैवावनिपालसङ्घैः। भीष्मो द्रोणः सूतपुत्रस्तथासौ सहास्मदीयैरपि योधमुख्यैः॥
transliteration: amī ca tvāṃ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putrāḥ sarve sahaivāvanipālasaṅghaiḥ | bhīṣmo droṇaḥ sūtaputrastathāsau sahāsmadīyairapi yodhamukhyaiḥ ||
literal_meaning: These sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, all of them, together with the hosts of kings, Bhīṣma, Droṇa, and that son of a charioteer (Karṇa), along with our chief warriors as well, are rushing into Your mouths, their heads smashed by Your fearful teeth.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna sees the totality of the battlefield—friends, foes, mentors, kin—being swallowed by Time. The vision collapses moral binaries: everyone is equally devoured, revealing the impersonal, inexorable process of psychic and physical annihilation.
psychological_state: Moral self-structure is shattered; ego experiences a ‘death by archetype’ as personal attachments are ground down by the autonomous force of Time. Arjuna confronts the Thanatos drive within himself and the cosmos.
trigger_scenario: A frontline medic or war journalist watches a drone-feed of an airstrike that kills both enemy combatants and embedded civilians—including their own platoon mates—realizing that survival is random and moral categories collapse under total war.
anchor_text: Everyone you love and fear is already being swallowed by time—act now, before grief paralyzes you.
